libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property appears necessary. For executable renderer coverage, create a temporary minimal skin JAR containing META-INF/maven/site.vm and obtain or initialize the renderer with its injected Doxia/Velocity/Plexus collaborators; direct use of new DefaultSiteRenderer() is only safe for methods that do not touch injected fields.

deterministic_setup.reason: The 2.0.0 public SiteRenderer API and skin-context path use org.apache.maven.artifact.Artifact, but the artifact declares maven-artifact as provided, so the normal test scaffold will not supply it.

The renderer discovers Doxia parser modules from the classpath; upstream 2.0.0 explicitly adds parser modules as test dependencies because they are not in doxia-core anymore. APT is the common Maven site document format and enables real render/locate coverage.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing 1.9.1 tests use APIs removed or renamed in 2.0.0, so they still need source changes; setup alone will not fix javac.
Parser-module coverage should be exercised by an actual render or locateDocumentFiles path; otherwise the added parser dependency may not improve reachability coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: doxia-site-renderer 2.0.0 moved Maven Artifact to provided scope and Doxia parsers are no longer supplied by core, so meaningful site-renderer coverage needs test-time support beyond the scaffold.
